claim: The official Slack integration only works in channels (not DMs), requires Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise with Claude Code Web access, supports GitHub-only repos, and limits each session to one PR; sessions count against the individual user's rate limits.
quote: Claude Code in Slack only works in channels (public or private). It does not work in direct messages (DMs). … GitHub only: Currently supports repositories on GitHub. One PR at a time: Each session can create one pull request. Rate limits apply: Sessions use your individual Claude plan's rate limits.

claim: The Slack surface is intentionally a thin trigger and notification layer — full transcript, file ops, and continuation live on Claude Code Web — so operators are pushed back to the browser for anything beyond a kickoff.
quote: In Slack, you'll see status updates, completion summaries, and action buttons. The full transcript is preserved and always accessible. … On the web: The complete Claude Code session with full conversation history, all code changes, file operations, and the ability to continue the session or create pull requests.

claim: Practitioners argue Slack is not the real dialog surface for Claude Code — it's mainly a kickoff button into the web session, which limits real collaboration and can't preview UI changes.
quote: Slack isn't the real 'dialog' surface. It's mainly to kick off the web-based Claude Code session. … Forcing the dialog into Claude Code also limits the opportunities for true collaboration. … Claude Code lacks a way to preview or render frontend changes, which is essential for ensuring high-quality PRs.

claim: Real-user write-ups list the same recurring complaints: GitHub-only, channels-only (no DMs), paid-tier gated, struggles on complex refactors, and produces nonsense on vague briefs.
quote: GitHub only. No GitLab, no Bitbucket, no self hosted repos. … Works in channels only. No direct messages. … struggles with complexity. Ask it to refactor your entire legacy monolith and it will get lost. … Vague requests like 'make the homepage better' produce nonsense.

claim: A live Anthropic issue (Claude Code 2.1.80) reports that `claude -p` headless sessions silently fail to load Claude AI connector MCPs — including the Slack connector — breaking any cron, LaunchAgent, or CI bridge that relies on `mcp__claude_ai_Slack__*`.
quote: claude -p headless sessions do not load Claude AI connector MCP servers… Tools like mcp__claude_ai_Slack__slack_send_message return NOT_FOUND when called in headless mode… This breaks any automation that uses claude -p (LaunchAgents, cron jobs, CI/CD) that depends on Claude AI connector MCPs.

claim: Long-running headless SDK sessions with parallel background subagents can hang because the Stop hook check finds zero hooks and never emits the result line, so a Slack bridge waiting on ResultMessage stalls without an error.
quote: the CLI never emits a {"type":"result",...} line to stdout after the final end_turn response. As a result, ResultMessage is never received by the SDK caller. … The CLI processes the Stop hook check and finds 0 matching hooks, then silently returns to waiting for the next user input without emitting the result line.

claim: From late March 2026, Max-tier users on Reddit and GitHub reported their 5-hour windows being burned in 90 minutes on previously-stable workloads — a runaway-cost class of bug that hits unattended Slack-driven sessions hardest.
quote: 5-hour session windows have been depleted within one to two hours using the same workloads that previously caused no problems. … Max 5x users claim their rate was spent after roughly 90 minutes. … usage jump from 21% to 100% on a single prompt.

claim: Community-built Slack bridges add what the official integration lacks: a policy engine, Block Kit approval buttons, multi-approver quorum, TTL-scoped approvals, and an explicit five-layer prompt-injection defense — signalling that out-of-the-box approval UX is insufficient for unattended use.
quote: permission relay with Block Kit buttons, five-layer prompt-injection defense … approvers: 2 setting requires two distinct Slack user IDs—preventing a single user from self-approving twice … System instructions explicitly tell Claude to refuse pairing/access requests from Slack messages.

claim: A widely-shared blog details a DIY pattern of running Claude Code in tmux under launchctl, polling Slack every minute to read threads, check the codebase, and reply on the author's behalf during a family holiday — illustrating that the away-from-desk use case requires hand-rolled scaffolding.
quote: Mine checks Slack every minute, reads threads, checks the codebase, and replies on my behalf while I'm on a family trip to Sydney.

claim: Issue #41424 captures the operator-side symptom of the metering bug: a fresh session at 3% context window already returns 'You're out of extra usage' — making Slack-kicked sessions unpredictably refuse to start.
quote: two separate Claude Code conversations hit rate limits almost immediately, despite showing only 32.3k / 1m tokens (3%) in context. … 'You're out of extra usage · resets 2pm (Asia/Riyadh)'.

claim: Anthropic publicly acknowledged tightening 5-hour session limits on Pro/Max during peak hours, conceding ~7% of users would now hit caps they wouldn't have before — confirming that cost-blowup complaints from unattended Slack runs map onto real policy changes, not just user error.
quote: adjusting our 5 hour session limits for free/Pro/Max subs during peak hours… ~7% of users will hit session limits they wouldn't have before, particularly for pro tiers.

claim: Round-trip latency from a phone through Anthropic's relay to a local Claude Code box is reported at 200–500 ms on good connections but several seconds on congested cellular — a meaningful tax on Slack-mediated approval loops.
quote: the round trip from your phone through Anthropic's relay to your local machine and back adds some latency, typically 200-500 milliseconds on a good connection but potentially several seconds on congested cellular networks.
